# Hermes Tag

> A Claude-Tag-style context-selection layer for the Hermes agent framework on Feishu/Lark (and Slack). @-mention it in a group and it answers in-thread from bounded per-chat memory and the right selected evidence — no full-history RAG, no ambient auto-answer. It overrides Hermes's built-in Feishu platform; it is not a new platform.

## Install (one command)
- `hermes plugins install DanielLi202/hermes-tag`
- Required env: `FEISHU_APP_ID`, `FEISHU_APP_SECRET` (Slack also needs `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN`, `SLACK_APP_TOKEN`).
- The one human, approval-gated step: an org admin must grant the sensitive Feishu scope `im:message.group_msg`. Everything else (install, config, restart, verify) an AI agent can do.

## For AI agents (start here)
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): the deterministic install + config recipe — exactly what an agent can do autonomously vs. what it must get from a human, a fill-in config template, and the acceptance checks to run before claiming success. Do not invent `oc_...`/`ou_...` ids or secrets; ask the human.

## Configure & onboard
- [after-install.md](after-install.md): two install paths (already-have-Hermes+Feishu vs. from scratch), env, minimal config, the full Feishu console setup, Slack setup, and live verification steps.

## Human-facing
- [README.md](README.md): what it is and the demo. (This is the only human marketing surface; the files above are written for agents.)
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): storage boundary, redacted audit, lifecycle controls.
- [docs/slack-setup.md](docs/slack-setup.md): Slack app manifest + deploy. Slack also requires running `docs/slack-manifest-add-tag.py` (ships in this repo) to inject `/tag` into the manifest — run it from the installed plugin's repo directory.

## Key facts an agent needs
- Feishu scopes: `im:message` (read messages + download media) and `im:message.group_msg` (receive all group messages — sensitive, admin-approval-gated; without it @-driven memory still works but unmentioned background context degrades).
- Config lives under `platforms.feishu.extra.feishu_tag` (and `platforms.slack.extra.slack_tag`). Set `require_mention: false` so unmentioned group messages reach Tier-0 buffering.
- Memory: Tier-0 = short-term per-chat buffer (TTL/count evicted); Tier-1 = long-term, built only from @-mention interactions, per-chat (never cross-chat).
- Group commands (require @-mention): `/tag status`, `/tag admin count|clear|disable|audit`, `/tag standing add|confirm|list|cancel|pause|enable`.
